risks.risk: AI Model Accuracy Below User Expectations
risks.mitigation: Implement progressive trust-building: start with suggestions, allow easy manual override, collect feedback to improve model. Provide transparent explanations for every priority score. Set launch bar at 75% user agreement rate rather than absolute accuracy.
risks.contingency: If accuracy stays below 60% agreement rate at beta, pivot to rule-based prioritization with AI as an optional enhancement layer.
triggers: Beta user feedback shows <65% agreement with AI priorities
Model evaluation metrics decline across 3 consecutive training runs
risks.description: The AI prioritization model may not achieve sufficient accuracy in real-world usage, leading to user frustration and low adoption of the core differentiating feature.

risks.mitigation: Pin API versions, implement adapter pattern for each integration, add health monitoring with automated alerts on sync failures, maintain a 2-version backward compatibility window.
risks.contingency: Gracefully degrade: disable affected integration with user notification, queue sync data for retry when API is stable.
triggers: Integration sync failure rate exceeds 5% for any provider
Provider announces deprecation of currently used API version
risks.description: External services (Slack, Google Calendar, Jira, GitHub) may introduce breaking API changes that disrupt the integration platform, affecting users who depend on data sync.

risks.mitigation: Speed to market with deep AI experience (not surface features). Build switching costs through personalized models that improve with usage. Focus on Focus Mode as a secondary differentiator that competitors are unlikely to prioritize.
risks.contingency: If a major competitor launches comparable AI features before our GA, pivot marketing to emphasize Focus Mode and privacy-first AI approach as differentiators.
triggers: Major competitor announces AI prioritization feature
Market research shows declining interest in standalone AI task tools
risks.description: Major task management platforms (Asana, Monday.com, ClickUp) may rapidly add AI prioritization features, reducing TaskFlow Pro's competitive differentiation window.

risks.mitigation: Implement data processing agreements, provide data residency options (US/EU), design architecture to support future on-premises ML inference. Complete GDPR Data Protection Impact Assessment before beta launch.
risks.contingency: Offer a 'privacy mode' that runs priority scoring using only metadata (due dates, labels, project) without analyzing task content text.
triggers: Enterprise prospect requests data residency guarantee we cannot provide
risks.description: Processing task content through AI models raises GDPR and CCPA concerns. Users in regulated industries may be prohibited from using cloud-based AI analysis of their work data.

risks.risk: Team Capacity Constraints Due to ML Specialization
risks.mitigation: Cross-train one backend engineer on ML inference serving. Document all model training procedures and evaluation metrics. Use well-established ML libraries to reduce custom code surface area.
risks.contingency: If ML engineer is unavailable for >2 weeks, contract a freelance ML engineer from pre-vetted pool. Existing model can serve inference without retraining.
triggers: ML engineer unavailable for more than 1 sprint
Model retraining required but no qualified person available
risks.description: Only one ML engineer (Dr. Patel) has the expertise to develop and maintain the AI prioritization model. If she is unavailable, EPIC-2 and related features will be severely delayed.
